title: 10 best herbal essential oils for enlarged spleen
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for enlarged spleen are a natural remedy that helps alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with an enlarged spleen. These oils are extracted from various herbs and plants and are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and improve overall health. The best herbal essential oils for enlarged spleen include Glycyrrhiza glabra, Curcuma longa, Echinacea purpurea, Ginkgo biloba, Zingiber officinale, Cinchona officinalis, Viscum album, Astragalus membranaceus, Rauvolfia serpentina, and Silybum marianum. If left untreated, an enlarged spleen can lead to severe abdominal pain, fatigue, and shortness of breath, significantly impacting one's quality of life and daily activities.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric essential oils helps with enlarged spleen because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive compound curcumin in turmeric oil has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to an enlarged spleen. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects can help regulate immune responses and promote overall liver health. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy liver function, turmeric essential oils may help alleviate symptoms associated with an enlarged spleen, such as discomfort, pain, and fatigue.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric essential oil has potent anti-inflammatory compounds like curcuminoids, which can help reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with an enlarged spleen.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of turmeric essential oil can help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to the enlargement of the spleen.

Immune-modulating: Turmeric essential oil has immunomodulatory effects, which means it can help regulate the immune system and prevent excessive inflammation or damage that may lead to an enlarged spleen.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce inflammation and swelling associated with an enlarged spleen.
Barks: Contain bioactive compounds like kaempferol and quercetin, which have been shown to inhibit inflammatory responses and alleviate splenomegaly symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh turmeric roots in the early morning mist and wash them gently with lukewarm water.
Dry the turmeric roots in shade for three hours or until they are brittle to the touch.
Steam distill 100g of dried turmeric roots in a stainless steel apparatus with 500ml of distilled water for one hour.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter paper into a clean glass bottle and store it in an airtight container.
Add 10 drops of the freshly prepared curcuma longa essential oil to a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil and massage onto the affected area.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with enlarged spleen because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. It contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have been shown to reduce swelling and inflammation in the body. Enlarged spleens are often caused by chronic infections or inflammatory conditions, and ginger oil's anti-inflammatory effects can help alleviate these issues. Additionally, ginger has been traditionally used to improve digestion and circulation, both of which can also contribute to an enlarged spleen.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger essential oil reduces inflammation, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with an enlarged spleen such as pain and swelling.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of ginger essential oil help protect the body from free radicals, which can contribute to oxidative stress and damage in the spleen, potentially reducing its size and improving function.

Digestive stimulating properties: Ginger essential oil has natural digestive stimulant properties that may help improve digestion, reduce bloating and discomfort, and alleviate symptoms associated with an enlarged spleen such as nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms associated with an enlarged spleen.
Barks: Containing bioactive compounds like sesquiterpenes, which may help reduce inflammation and improve overall liver function, benefiting the spleen's health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes weighing approximately 100 grams.
Dry the harvested rhizomes in a warm place for at least three days.
Steam distill the dried rhizomes using 20% distillation ratio and 4 hours of steam time.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil into a dark glass bottle.
Store the freshly prepared zingiber officinale essential oil in a cool dry place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with enlarged spleen because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the spleen and surrounding tissues, which could potentially alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain, swelling, and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of peppermint contain a high concentration of menthol, which helps to reduce inflammation and relax the digestive system, making it beneficial for relieving symptoms of an enlarged spleen.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality peppermint leaves from your garden or purchase them from a reputable source in sufficient quantities.
Rinse the peppermint leaves thoroughly with clean water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine one tablespoon of fresh peppermint leaves with eight ounces of boiling water and let it steep for five minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the solids.
Drink the tea immediately and repeat as needed up to three times per day, ideally after meals.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of cymbopogon citratus and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the washed leaves in a single layer on paper towels or clean cloths for at least 24 hours.
Crush dried cymbopogon citratus leaves using mortar and pestle to release their essential oils and flavors.
Steam distill crushed cymbopogon citratus material in a stainless steel apparatus with deionized water at atmospheric pressure.
Collect and separate the resulting essential oil from the distilled water using separation funnels or decantation methods.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus leaves at dawn or dusk for optimal potency and oil content.
Clean and dry the harvested eucalyptus leaves by gently shaking off excess moisture and patting dry with paper towels.
Steam distill 100g of dried eucalyptus leaves in 1 liter of boiling water for 2 hours to extract essential oils.
Filter the resulting oil mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and retaining the clear liquid.
Store the filtered herbal eucalyptus globulus essential oil in an amber glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature for up to 6 months.
